title: Add generic dependencies
body:
Right now, libpod has a notion of dependencies between containers - if container A uses container B's network namespace, container B must be running before container A can start so we can join that namespace. These patches extend this notion of dependencies to be generic - we can now make a container depend on other containers, only starting when these other containers are started. This allows enforced ordering of container start.

I'm working on patches to add a `--recursive` flag to `start` and `remove` to take advantage of this, starting a container and all dependencies that are not running, and removing a container and all its dependencies. Those will come in a separate PR so as to not clutter things.

comments.body:
Docker allows you to remove the container you depend on. If you start the container with the dependency again, it will fail, saying the container it depends on no longer exists.

We treat dependencies a little more seriously (if the CRI-O pause container gets deleted by someone fiddling with `podman rm` that's not a good thing), so we won't let you remove the container if things depend on it, but this does cause issues (`rm -f` no longer guarantees a container is gone). I think a `recursive` flag is a good solution here, because it makes it clear other containers may be gone after you remove this one.

I believe -f should imply --recursive.  If I say I want a container or Pod gone and I say force it, then all of the containers that rely on the container/pod should be removed also.

Adding a --recursive flag just complicates it.

@rhatdan Dropped the commit with the CLI parts. There are still a few changes to `podman run` and `podman start`, but it's mostly simplifying things by removing `Init()` before `Start()`.

On --force implying recursive: I don't know if I agree, but we can have that discussion once the code is actually written...
